LAW AND MOTION 302,NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF BALANCE STUDIO, INC.'S MOTION TO FILE PLAINTIFFS ***UNREDACTED*** OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS DEMURRER UNDER SEAL. PLAINTIFF BALANCE STUDIO, INC.'S MOTION TO FILE UNREDACTED OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER UNDER SEAL IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION FILED AND GOOD CAUSE SHOWN PURSUANT TO CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, RULES 2.550 AND 2.551. THE COURT FINDS THAT THERE EXISTS AN OVERRIDING INTEREST THAT OVERCOMES THE RIGHT OF PUBLIC ACCESS TO THE PORTIONS OF THE RECORDS TO BE SEALED; THE OVERRIDING INTEREST SUPPORTS SEALING THOSE RECORDS; A SUBSTANTIAL PROBABILITY EXISTS THAT THE OVERRIDING INTEREST WILL BE PREJUDICED IF THE RECORD IS NOT SEALED; THE PROPOSED SEALING IS NARROWLY TAILORED; AND NO LESS RESTRICTIVE MEANS EXIST TO ACHIEVE THE OVERRIDING INTEREST. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

Application For Order To File Unredacted Demurrer Under Seal
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC'S Application For an order to seal an unredacted Version of Defendant's Demurrer to Plaintiff's Complaint.

Defendant Cybernet Entertainment LLC's application to file unredacted versions of defendant's demurrer and reply brief under seal is granted. No opposition filed and good cause shown pursuant to California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Motion To File Plaintiffs ***Unredacted*** Opposition To Defendants Demurrer Under Seal
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's GENERAL DEMURRERS TO COMPLAINT.

Defendant Cybernet Entertainment LLC's demurrer to the complaint filed by plaintiff Balance Studio Inc. is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract, sustained with 20 days leave to amend as to the second cause of action for intentional misrepresentation, and sustained without leave to amend as to the third and fourth causes of action for negligent misrepresentation and for preliminary and permanent injunction.

Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for breach of contract because plaintiff's interpretation of the two prohibitions in para. 4, read in conjunction with para. 5 of the agreement, is not unreasonable and should be accepted by the court on demurrer. "'So long as the pleading does not place a clearly erroneous construction upon the provisions of the contract, in passing upon the sufficiency of the complaint, we must accept as correct plaintiff's allegations as to the meaning of the agreement.'" (Klein v. Chevron U.S.A., Inc. (2012) 202 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1385; see also Fire Ins. Exchange v. Superior Court (2004) 116 Cal.App.4th 446, 452 ["Ordinarily, a general demurrer does not lie as to a portion of a cause of action, and if any part of a cause of action is properly pleaded, the demurrer will be overruled."].)

The fraudulent misrepresentation/promissory fraud cause of action is not barred by the economic loss rule. (See Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 551-552 ["conduct amounting to a breach of contract becomes tortious only when it also violates a duty independent of the contract arising from principles of tort law. . . Tort damages have been permitted in contract cases . . . where the contract was fraudulently induced."]). The demurer is sustained because plaintiff has failed to allege promissory fraud with the requisite specificity. (See Rossberg v. Bank of America, N.A. (2013) 219 Cal.App.4th 1481, 1498 [each element of a promissory fraud claim must be alleged with particularity]; Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157 ["The requirement of specificity in a fraud action against a corporation requires the plaintiff to allege the names of the persons who made the allegedly fraudulent representations, their authority to speak, to whom they spoke, what they said or wrote, and when it was said or written."].) Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to cure that deficiency.

The negligent misrepresentation cause of action is not supported by the allegations, which do not refer to past or present statements of fact but only to a false promise. There is no cause of action for a negligent false promise. (Tarmann v. State Farm Mut. Auto. Ins. Co. (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 159.) Because plaintiff has not shown how amendment will cure that deficiency, the demurrer to this cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

Because plaintiff has not opposed defendant's demurrer to the cause of action for injunctive relief, and because injunctive relief is a remedy and not a cause of action in itself, the demurrer to the fourth cause of action is sustained without leave to amend.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
